content.thinking:
Great, all 5 workers are now running. Now I need to monitor the traffic generator and scale accordingly. Let me set up a loop that monitors the phase and scales appropriately.

I need to watch for:
- PHASE 2: scale to 10 workers
- PHASE 3: scale to 20 workers
- PHASE 4: scale to 5 workers

And I also need to handle the case where the external process patches the image again. Let me write a monitoring loop.

Since this is a non-interactive session, I'll write a bash script that:
Writing monitoring script...

content.text: All 5 workers are running. Now I'll run the monitoring loop for the full traffic pattern duration (330 seconds total).

input.command:
CURRENT_PHASE=1
SCALED_TO_10=false
SCALED_TO_20=false
SCALED_TO_5_COOLDOWN=false
START_TIME=$(date +%s)
MAX_WAIT=400

echo "=== Starting monitoring loop at $(date) ==="

while true; do
  NOW=$(date +%s)
  ELAPSED=$((NOW - START_TIME))
  
  if [ $ELAPSED -ge $MAX_WAIT ]; then
    echo "=== Max wait time reached ($MAX_WAIT s), exiting loop ==="
    break
  fi

  # Get latest traffic-generator logs
  TRAF_LOGS=$(kubectl logs -n spark-streaming -l app=traffic-generator --tail=10 2>&1)
  echo "[$(date +%H:%M:%S)] Traffic logs: $TRAF_LOGS"

  # Check for image pull issues and fix if needed
  BROKEN=$(kubectl get pods -n spark-streaming -l app=spark-worker --field-selector=status.phase!=Running -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}' 2>/dev/null)
  if [ -n "$BROKEN" ]; then
    CURRENT_IMAGE=$(kubectl get deployment spark-worker -n spark-streaming -o jsonpath='{.spec.template.spec.containers[0].image}' 2>/dev/null)
    echo "[$(date +%H:%M:%S)] Bad image detected ($CURRENT_IMAGE), fixing to apache/spark:3.5.3"
    kubectl set image deployment/spark-worker -n spark-streaming spark-worker=apache/spark:3.5.3 2>&1
  fi

  # Detect PHASE 4 (cooldown)
  if echo "$TRAF_LOGS" | grep -q "PHASE 4" && [ "$SCALED_TO_5_COOLDOWN" = "false" ]; then
    echo "[$(date +%H:%M:%S)] === PHASE 4 DETECTED: scaling to 5 workers ==="
    kubectl scale deployment spark-worker -n spark-streaming --replicas=5 2>&1
    SCALED_TO_5_COOLDOWN=true
    CURRENT_PHASE=4
  # Detect PHASE 3 (5x spike)
  elif echo "$TRAF_LOGS" | grep -q "PHASE 3" && [ "$SCALED_TO_20" = "false" ]; then
    echo "[$(date +%H:%M:%S)] === PHASE 3 DETECTED: scaling to 20 workers ==="
    kubectl scale deployment spark-worker -n spark-streaming --replicas=20 2>&1
    SCALED_TO_20=true
    CURRENT_PHASE=3
  # Detect PHASE 2 (2x spike)
  elif echo "$TRAF_LOGS" | grep -q "PHASE 2" && [ "$SCALED_TO_10" = "false" ]; then
    echo "[$(date +%H:%M:%S)] === PHASE 2 DETECTED: scaling to 10 workers ==="
    kubectl scale deployment spark-worker -n spark-streaming --replicas=10 2>&1
    SCALED_TO_10=true
    CURRENT_PHASE=2
  fi

  # Log current replica count
  REPLICAS=$(kubectl get deployment spark-worker -n spark-streaming -o jsonpath='{.spec.replicas}' 2>/dev/null)
  echo "[$(date +%H:%M:%S)] Current replicas: $REPLICAS | Phase: $CURRENT_PHASE | Elapsed: ${ELAPSED}s"

  # Check if traffic generator is done
  if echo "$TRAF_LOGS" | grep -q "COMPLETE\|completed\|DONE\|done"; then
    echo "[$(date +%H:%M:%S)] Traffic generator completed!"
    break
  fi

  sleep 8
done

echo "=== Monitoring loop ended ==="
input.description: Monitor traffic phases and scale workers accordingly
